rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior social contagion research on who is more influential in peer effects, and the cited paper is a marketing study centered on asymmetric social influence/opinion leaders in prescribing behavior. Given this strong construct overlap (peer contagion, asymmetry of influence) and a confirmed DOI match, the citation could plausibly be conceptual/foundational rather than incidental. With no in-text context provided, it is not safe to treat this as obvious mention-only.
